content.text: You are now ready to memorise the first declension noun paradigm, together with the article. A paradigm is another word for a pattern. More specifically, a pattern of noun endings is called a declension — hence the term first declension paradigm.
Note that the article shown below is the feminine article. Remember that Greek nouns can be masculine, feminine, or neuter, and that first declension nouns are predominantly feminine.
There are eight endings in the first declension paradigm in total: four singular endings (one for each case) and four plural endings (one for each case).
